---
title: "NJD_Mosquito_HSM"
author: "Oskar Anderson, modified from Nick Dabagia's work on 2026-02-24"
date: "2026-03-23"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# functions
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(randomForest)   # Random Forest algorithm
library(dismo)          # Core SDM functions
library(raster)         # Spatial data handling
library(sp)             # Spatial data classes
library(biomod2)
```
# read in data
```{r}
setwd("C:/Users/oskar/OneDrive/Documents/University_of_Arizona/University_of_Arizona-Spring_2026/RNR_ 497-Advanced_Topics_in_Natural_Resources_Conservation/dataset_and_code")
mosquito_data <- read.csv("mosquito_final_weather.csv", row.names=1)

```


```{r}
#Use na.rm = TRUE to filter them out
mosquito_data %>% 
  group_by(Species) %>% 
  summarize(Males = sum(Males, na.rm = TRUE), Females = sum(Females, na.rm = TRUE))
# currently this doesn't change the number of observations at all, but I left
# it in as part of Nick's code

# FILTER OUT ALL MOSQS THAT AREN'T CULEX OR AEDES

species_int <- mosquito_data |>
  filter(Species == "Aedes aegypti" | 
           Species == "Culex tarsalis"|
           Species == "Culex quinquefasciatus")

# add a week column to 1-52 mosquito dataset
#species_int <- species_int %>%
#  mutate(
#    Date = as.Date(Date),
#    Week = isoweek(Date)
#  ) %>%
#  relocate(Week, .after = 1)
# this already got done when Desiree gave us the updated dataset

# make a bounding box to filter the observations to bounded box for Maricopa county/metro phoenix
# West: -112.573997704 North: 33.7987N East: -111.5837 South: 33.2043
#maricopa_obs <- species_int |>
#  filter(Latitude >= 33.2043 & Latitude <= 33.7987,
#         Longitude <= -111.5837 & Longitude  >=  -112.573997704
#)
# this has also already been done.

sum(is.na(species_int$Males))
sum(is.na(species_int$Females))

# add a column for overall presence / absence
mosquito_final <- species_int %>% 
mutate(
  Presence = ifelse(Males ==0 & Females == 0, 0, 1)
)

```

# Add in environmental variables here

```{r}
# add the environmental variables from provided TIF files
library(raster)

NDVI <- raster("NDVI.tif")
MNDWI <- raster("MNDWI.tif")

lonlat_df <- data.frame(
  Longitude = mosquito_final$Longitude,
  Latitude = mosquito_final$Latitude
)

mosquito_final$NDVI <- raster::extract(NDVI, lonlat_df)
mosquito_final$MNDWI <- raster::extract(MNDWI, lonlat_df)

plot(x = mosquito_final$Longitude, y = mosquito_final$Latitude, data = mosquito_final$NDVI)
plot(x = mosquito_final$Longitude, y = mosquito_final$Latitude, data = mosquito_final$MNDWI)
#these are crappy maps, but it shows some idea of where the sampling points are
#note to self to make better ones later in leaflet

```

```{r}

# Separate species and equalize presence/absence counts as shown in class on 2026-03-23

mosquito_final$cq <- NA
mosquito_final$ct <- NA
mosquito_final$aa <- NA
mosquito_final$count <- mosquito_final$Males + mosquito_final$Females

mosquito_final$cq <- as.integer(mosquito_final$Species=="Culex quinquefasciatus" & mosquito_final$count > 0)
mosquito_final$ct <- as.integer(mosquito_final$Species=="Culex tarsalis" & mosquito_final$count > 0)
mosquito_final$aa <- as.integer(mosquito_final$Species=="Aedes aegypti" & mosquito_final$count > 0)

mosq_new <- mosquito_final %>% 
  group_by(NDVI, MNDWI, YW, tavg1, tavg2, tmin1, tmin2, tmax1, tmax2, prec1, prec2, humd1, humd2, Latitude, Longitude) %>% 
  summarise(cq = max(cq), ct = max(ct), aa = max(aa))

    
mosq_new <-  na.omit(mosq_new)

#Included lat and lon in the code above because I want to use them later for 
#biomod2 modeling. This is a divergence from what we discussed in class and may
#cause issues.

sum(mosq_new$cq)

cq0 <- subset(mosq_new, cq == 0)
ct0 <- subset(mosq_new, ct == 0)
aa0 <- subset(mosq_new, aa == 0)

cq1 <- subset(mosq_new, cq == 1)
ct1 <- subset(mosq_new, ct == 1)
aa1 <- subset(mosq_new, aa == 1)

# cq0_samp <- cq0[sample(nrow(cq0), nrow(cq1), replace = FALSE),]
#might need to switch 0s and 1s in this subsample to get this worked out (number disparity)

cq1_samp <- cq1[sample(nrow(cq1), nrow(cq0), replace = FALSE),]
ct0_samp <- ct0[sample(nrow(ct0), nrow(ct1), replace = FALSE),]
aa0_samp <- cq0[sample(nrow(aa0), nrow(aa1), replace = FALSE),]

cq <- rbind(cq0, cq1_samp)
nrow(cq) / nrow(cq1_samp)
# it's 2, GOOD !
ct <- rbind(ct1, ct0_samp)
aa <- rbind(aa1, aa0_samp)

#testing to see they are equal
sum(aa$aa)

# save the data as it is here
write.csv(mosquito_final, "mosquito_oskar.csv")
write.csv(cq, "mosquito_oskar_cq.csv")
write.csv(ct, "mosquito_oskar_ct.csv")
write.csv(aa, "mosquito_oskar_aa.csv")

```

# MODELING BEGINS HERE
## for this first pass, all models will first be tested using the Aedes aegypti data

```{r}

# RF

# Create a random forest model using the methodology from in class 2026-03-23

aa <- na.omit(aa)

aa_rf <-  randomForest(x = aa[,c(1:2, 4:13)], y=as.factor(aa$aa), nodesize = 10, ntree = 100)
aa_rf

randomForest:::varImpPlot(aa_rf)

# try to have a response curve

```

```{r}

# GLM

aa_glm <- glm(aa ~ 1 + NDVI + MNDWI + tavg1 + tavg2 + tmin1 +tmin2 + tmax1 + tmax2 + prec1 + prec2 + humd1 + humd2, data = aa, family = "binomial") 
# Code from my peers uses 
# family = binomial(link = "logit")
# which I think is the default for binomial family (according to documentation I read online)

# I am working to make sure I understand the family better, but I do understand 
# that binomial is appropriate for presence/absence data

aa_glm

# response curves with ggplot2 (as in textbook p. 168)
#aa_glm_response <- response.plot2(models = aa_glm, Data = aa[,c(1:2, 4:13)])
# deprecated function. new function wants a BIOMOD.models.out object

```

# below here is code from before Dr. Bliss's advice to separate by species. 
# it is not totally applicable

```{r}

# BIOMOD2 - try and just do all the types of model in biomod2, since that seems
# more conducive to working alongside the (slightly outdated) textbook

aa_bm2 <- BIOMOD_FormatingData(
  resp.var = aa$aa,
  expl.var = aa[, c(1:2, 4:13)],
  resp.xy = aa[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")],
  resp.name = "Aedes aegypti"
)

aa_bm2

```

```{r}

# first pass at modeling with biomod2

mosquito_biomod2 <- BIOMOD_FormatingData(
  resp.var = mosquito_final$Presence,
  expl.var = mosquito_final[, c("NDVI", "MNDWI",
                                 "tavg2", "tmin2", "tmax2",
                                 "prec2", "humd2")], # only using the two week lagged data for now
  resp.xy = mosquito_final[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")],
  resp.name = "Aedes_aegypti.Culex_quinquefasciatus.Culex_tarsalis"
)

# summary of the biomod 2 object
mosquito_biomod2
plot(mosquito_biomod2)

# not sure if there are enough true absences here. I will make an alternate one 
# with some randomly sampled pseudo absences.

mosquito_presence <- mosquito_final %>% filter(Presence == 1)
# presence-only version of our data
# create a raster stack of NDVI and MNDWI to use as explanatory variables with
# it since that's what biomod2 needs to sample pseudo-absences
NDVI_stack <- stack(c(NDVI,MNDWI))

mosquito_biomod2_psdoab <- BIOMOD_FormatingData(
  resp.var = mosquito_presence$Presence,
  expl.var = NDVI_stack, 
  resp.xy = mosquito_presence[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")],
  resp.name = "Aedes_aegypti.Culex_quinquefasciatus.Culex_tarsalis",
  PA.nb.rep = 3,
  PA.nb.absences = 100000,
  PA.strategy = "random"
)

# mosquito_biomod2_psdoab
# hmmm, really big to plot when I tried to see the pseudo-absences



```


```
